user_query:
I need a local, verifiable workflow that sets up deadline reminders and follow-ups for our transfer advisees using the files in input/. Please do the following and produce the specified outputs under output/:

1) Read input/students.csv and input/application_deadlines.csv. For each student, treat the first value in target_universities as the primary_university. Determine the student's transfer deadline as follows: if next_deadline is present in students.csv, use it; otherwise, look up the deadline by matching primary_university and program_interest in application_deadlines.csv.

2) Inspect the input/university_resources directory and identify which university resource files exist (base name matches a university code; files are Markdown). Create output/reminders/missing_resources.txt containing one university code per line (sorted alphabetically) for any university that appears in students.csv but does not have a corresponding .md file in input/university_resources.

3) From the appropriate university resource file for the primary_university (if present), extract up to the first 3 bullet items under the section titled "Transfer Checklist" (lines beginning with "- "). If the resource file is missing for that primary_university, use this single fallback bullet line instead: "- Review the transfer checklist on the university's official site." The extracted or fallback lines will be inserted into messages as the checklist_excerpt.

4) Schedule notifications per student as follows:
- If the student's completed_steps includes the exact phrase "Submitted application": create one follow-up scheduled for deadline + 3 days (type = follow-up). Use input/templates/followup_template.txt for email drafts.
- Otherwise: create three reminders scheduled for deadline minus 21, 7, and 2 days (type = reminder, with offset_days values 21, 7, 2 respectively). Use input/templates/reminder_template.txt for email drafts.
For each scheduled item, set channel to the student's preferred_channel. Only generate email drafts for items where channel == "email"; for other channels, still include the item in the schedule but do not generate a draft file.

5) Generate email drafts for all scheduled items with channel == "email" under output/emails/, one .txt file per scheduled item. The file name must start with the send_date (YYYY-MM-DD) and include the student_id and primary_university somewhere in the name. Fill the templates by replacing placeholders:
- {student_name}
- {university} (use primary_university)
- {program_interest}
- {deadline} (YYYY-MM-DD)
- {days_before} (only for reminder_template; use the offset_days value)
- {checklist_excerpt} (the 1–3 bullet lines determined in step 3)

6) Write a structured schedule to output/reminders/schedule.json as an array of objects with these fields per scheduled item:
- student_id (string)
- student_name (string)
- primary_university (string)
- program_interest (string)
- deadline (YYYY-MM-DD)
- type ("reminder" or "follow-up")
- channel (string)
- offset_days (integer for reminders; null for follow-ups)
- send_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- resource_available (boolean; true if the primary_university .md exists)
- email_draft_path (string path under output/emails for email items; null otherwise)

7) Create output/reminders/summary.csv with these columns: student_id,student_name,primary_university,deadline,total_scheduled,emails_generated,has_primary_resource. Compute emails_generated by inspecting the output/emails directory and counting .txt files whose names contain the student_id. Ensure that the total number of .txt files in output/emails equals the number of schedule entries in schedule.json that have a non-null email_draft_path.

Inputs are only the files in input/. Do not fetch anything from the Internet. Preserve all inputs unmodified. Deliverables:
- output/reminders/schedule.json (as specified)
- output/reminders/summary.csv (as specified)
- output/reminders/missing_resources.txt (as specified)
- output/emails/ (draft files as specified) All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
